violations.text: The hand-washing sink in the meat area was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/03/2024

Potato salad packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024
Walk-in cooler door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

The meat area does not have a hand-washing sink that is conveniently located and/or readily accessible. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Raw animal foods (beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (soup) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Chicken wings and empanadas were observed held at 107-111 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Pert interview with PIC, foods were made less than 2 hours prior. PIC reheated at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Soup, beans and beef were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Several food items were observed cooling on the counter in covered containers greater than 4 inches deep. PIC moved to smaller containers, moved to walk in cooler. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024

Potato salad and tamales packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024
Walk-in cooler door gaskets were observed torn and in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/04/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 11/20/2024
